summary: Request to make the Dispose method virtual in SKGLView (Blazor) so subclasses can override disposal logic; the same non-virtual Dispose pattern also affects SKCanvasView.

stepsToReproduce: Subclass SKGLView in a Blazor WASM project
Attempt to override Dispose() to add custom cleanup logic
Observe that Dispose is not virtual and cannot be overridden

analysis.summary: SKGLView.Dispose() is declared as a plain non-virtual public method implementing IDisposable. The class is not sealed, so subclassing is valid, but subclasses cannot override Dispose to add their own cleanup. SKCanvasView has the same pattern. The standard .NET approach would be the Dispose(bool) pattern with a protected virtual method.
analysis.rationale: This is an enhancement to existing functionality — the Dispose infrastructure exists but doesn't follow the standard .NET virtual Dispose(bool) pattern. Not a feature request (no new functionality) and not a bug (it's a design limitation, not broken behavior). The reporter explicitly tags it as [FEATURE] and describes the subclassing use case.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKGLView implements IDisposable with a non-virtual public Dispose() method. The class is not sealed, allowing subclasses, but Dispose cannot be overridden. Additionally, the Dispose method does not use null-conditional operators (unlike SKCanvasView), and does not dispose GL resources (context, glInterface, renderTarget, surface).

SKCanvasView has the same non-virtual Dispose() pattern. It does use null-conditional operators and additionally calls FreeBitmap(). Both views would benefit from the same Dispose(bool) pattern change.

keySignals.text: SKGLView in blazor implementation has a non-virtual Dispose method, which creates problems when subclassing it.
keySignals.interpretation: Clear description of the limitation — the reporter is subclassing SKGLView and needs to add custom disposal logic.
Reporter links directly to the Dispose method. The file path has since been restructured to source/SkiaSharp.Views/SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor/ but the code is unchanged.

resolution.hypothesis: The Blazor views implement IDisposable directly without the standard Dispose(bool) virtual pattern, preventing subclasses from extending disposal.

proposals.title: Implement Dispose(bool) pattern
proposals.description: Refactor both SKGLView and SKCanvasView to use the standard .NET Dispose(bool disposing) pattern: add a protected virtual Dispose(bool) method containing the cleanup logic, and have the public Dispose() call Dispose(true). This allows subclasses to override Dispose(bool) and call base.Dispose(disposing).

proposals.title: Use 'new' keyword to hide Dispose
proposals.description: As a workaround, subclasses can declare 'public new void Dispose()' to hide the base Dispose and call base disposal manually. This does not provide polymorphic disposal (callers holding an SKGLView reference will call the base Dispose), but works when the subclass type is known at the call site.

resolution.recommendedReason: Follows the standard .NET IDisposable pattern, enables proper subclass disposal, and is a small, low-risk change. The workaround using 'new' is fragile and breaks polymorphic disposal.

actionability.reason: Well-specified enhancement request with clear scope. Needs implementation and testing to ensure the Dispose(bool) pattern doesn't break existing Blazor component lifecycle behavior.

actions.description: Apply enhancement, Blazor views, and WASM labels
Acknowledge the request and provide the workaround
